---
title: "Noticing Alcohol Scripts"
format: html
---

# Noticing Alcohol Scripts

An *alcohol script* is an automatic set of thoughts, expectations, or beliefs about drinking in a specific situation. These scripts develop over time through repetition, social influence, and past experiences.

In high-risk environments (like bars or parties), scripts can activate quickly and feel “true” without being consciously examined. Increasing awareness of these scripts is the first step toward changing behavior.

![](https://github.com/jjcurtin/aud_support/blob/main/modules/images/script.png?raw=true){fig-align="center"}

## Work through this worksheet to identify the automatic thoughts and expectations that show up in risky environments.

### 1. Identify the Situation

-   **Risky environment:**\
    \>

-   **When do you go there?**\
    \>

-   **Who are you usually with?**\
    \>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 2. Recognize the Script

What thoughts or expectations come up automatically?

Check any that apply:

-   [ ] “Drinking is expected here.”\
-   [ ] “It won’t be fun if I don’t drink.”\
-   [ ] “Everyone else is drinking.”\
-   [ ] “I’ll feel more comfortable if I drink.”\
-   [ ] “Just one won’t hurt.”\
-   [ ] “I deserve this.”\
-   [ ] Other: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 3. Write Your Script

Put your typical thought into one sentence:

> 

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 4. Notice the Impact

-   When this thought shows up, what do you tend to do?\
    \>

-   How strong is the urge that follows (0–10)? **\_\_\_\_\_**

------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: callout
### Quick Reflection

-   Does this script feel automatic?\
-   Does it feel true in the moment?
:::

### Remember: Awareness creates a pause. That pause creates choice.
